text: Science has a clearer story about phytoplankton blooms than social media does. These tiny organisms are the backbone of marine ecosystems, producing half of the world's oxygen. But what happens when they bloom excessively? It's a double-edged sword. While blooms can boost fish populations, they can also deplete oxygen, leading to dead zones. Understanding these blooms helps us manage our oceans better and protect marine life. Curious about more ocean mysteries? Hit that follow button!
